bolt.directive: Maintain the current performance baseline perfectly. Do not implement any new optimization tasks. Defer all performance enhancements (including subshell removals) until the critical validation debts from Palette and Sentinel regarding systemd sandboxing are fully resolved and system stability is definitively confirmed.

palette.directive: URGENT: Execute the pending validation task immediately. Thoroughly verify that journal logs remain clear, accessible, and actionable for the strictly sandboxed services (`neos-autoupdate.service`). Ensure that failures caused by read-only filesystem restrictions are explicitly bubbled up in `journalctl` and not masked by generic exit codes (like 1 or 127), which would degrade debugging capabilities. Also ensure failures in unsandboxed `neos-liveuser-setup.service` remain readable and actionable.

sentinel.directive: URGENT: Execute the pending validation task immediately. Conduct a mathematically rigorous security audit of the strict systemd sandboxing configurations in `profile/airootfs/etc/systemd/system/neos-autoupdate.service`. Ensure zero unnecessary privileges are granted while confirming `ReadWritePaths` remain minimal but functionally adequate. Definitively validate that autoupdaters retain access to `/var/lib/pacman`, `/usr`, `/boot`, `/opt`, and `/srv`. Confirm `CAP_SYS_MODULE` is preserved in `CapabilityBoundingSet` for kernel module hooks. Prevent any systemic breakage from `ProtectSystem=strict`. In addition, strictly verify that `profile/airootfs/etc/systemd/system/neos-liveuser-setup.service` remains unsandboxed so that essential commands such as `useradd -m` (writing to `/etc` and `/home`) can complete successfully without permission errors.

note: Critical, immediate directives mandated by the No-build day (strategic pause) to clear accumulated validation debt from recent strict sandboxing. No further development or architectural shifts will proceed until these tasks are verified as 100% complete and fully audited by Sentinel and Palette.
